TextView.android:text: Community-powered reporting for safer roads, environments and public spaces.

Hazentra is a mobile crowdsourcing application that enables users to report road, environmental and building hazards. Users may submit hazard information using their current GPS location or manually entered coordinates. Active hazard reports are downloaded dynamically from the server and displayed as categorized markers on Google Maps.
Detects the user's real-time GPS coordinates.
Downloads active hazard locations directly from the server.
Allows users to submit reports to a PHP and MySQL server.
Supports report searching, filtering and status management.
The four developers responsible for designing, developing and presenting Hazentra.
Android interface development, Google Maps integration, current-location detection, hazard-report form and mobile application testing.
PHP API development, server request validation, API testing and Android-to-server communication.
MySQL database development, admin-dashboard interface, report filtering and hazard-record management.
Functional testing, interface evaluation, written documentation, GitHub management and YouTube demonstration.
Source code, server files and documentation
Access the Hazentra Android application, PHP server files, MySQL database script and project documentation through the official GitHub repository.
Developed for ICT602 Mobile Technology and Development Universiti Teknologi MARA
